topic: A chemist performing a 'Titration' until the exact 'End Point' is reached.

parsed_generation.script:
Scene 1 — Opening (0-2s): Side view of the chemist at a clean bench; macro close-up on a burette mounted above a conical flask; hand steadies the setup as the titrant is prepared.
Scene 2 — Action (2-5s): Over-the-shoulder cam shows the chemist's hands; the stopcock is twisted, a fine stream enters the flask; the indicator is present and the solution color begins to transition.
Scene 3 — End Point (5-8s): Extreme close-up of the flask; the indicator turns pink and remains, signifying End Point reached; the burette reading is steady.
parsed_generation.narration: A titration measures how much titrant is needed to reach the endpoint. The indicator’s color change marks the exact moment the reaction is complete. This sequence highlights precision, control, and accurate readings. The final data from the burette enables concentration calculation.

feedback: The sequence stays focused on a chemist performing a titration to the end point and includes concrete endpoint cues. To align more closely with the concise, action-first style of the references, tighten the phrasing to shorter, direct prompts and avoid extraneous framing like multiple scene labels. Add explicit, precise readouts (e.g., the exact burette measurement) to reinforce the endpoint precision.
strengths: Clear focus on titration and endpoint moment
Varied shot types (side view, over-the-shoulder, macro) convey action
fixes: Adopt a more concise, action-first prompt style similar to reference examples
Include a precise numerical endpoint reading to emphasize accuracy (e.g., 'Endpoint at 25.60 mL')
